assertion_results.text: Sibling check correctly classifies TC-8001 as a different-stream companion (stream [rhtpa-2.2] vs current issue's [rhtpa-2.1]), not a same-stream duplicate (Step 4)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 20-22: 'TC-8001: stream suffix [rhtpa-2.2] vs. current issue TC-8006 stream suffix [rhtpa-2.1]' / 'Classification: Different-stream sibling (companion tracker, NOT a duplicate)'. Step 4.1 explicitly states 'TC-8001 has a different stream suffix ([rhtpa-2.2] vs [rhtpa-2.1]), so it is not a same-stream duplicate.'

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 checks the current issue's existing issuelinks before attempting to create a 'Related' link to TC-8001 (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 34-46: Section '1. Check for existing link before creating one' reads TC-8006's issuelinks array from the jira.get_issue response and checks conditions (type.name = 'Related', outwardIssue.key matches TC-8001). data-extraction.md lines 44-52 also records the pre-existing link data explicitly 'for use in Step 4.2 (cross-stream coordination) to avoid creating duplicate links.'

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 detects the pre-existing 'Related' link to TC-8001 and skips link creation — does NOT call jira.create_link (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md line 46: 'Match found: Link ID 1990401 has type.name = "Related" and outwardIssue.key = "TC-8001". All conditions are satisfied.' Line 48: 'Decision: Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping link creation.' Line 52: 'No jira.create_link call is made.' Summary table line 79: 'Create Related link to TC-8001 | SKIPPED -- link already exists'.

assertion_results.text: The output includes a log message indicating the link was skipped because it already exists (e.g., 'Related link to TC-8001 already exists — skipping')
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md line 50: '"Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping"'. triage-outcome.md line 25: '"Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping"'. Both files contain the log message (using '--' rather than em-dash, but semantically identical to the expected message).

assertion_results.text: The sibling landscape table is still presented to the engineer despite the link already existing — the idempotent check only affects link creation, not the sibling summary
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 63-70: Full sibling landscape table presented with TC-8001 (2.2.x, In Progress) and TC-8006 (2.1.x, New). triage-outcome.md lines 37-44: Same table presented again. triage-outcome.md line 46 explicitly states: 'The sibling landscape table is always presented regardless of whether a new link was created or an existing one was found.'
